pr.title: [WIP] Add queue and batch processing features for library automation
pr.body:
Thanks for asking me to work on this. I will get started on it and keep this PR's description up to date as I form a plan and make progress.

> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[EPIC] Add queue, batch processing, and library-scale automation</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Goal
> Turn the project into a real library-processing platform.
> 
> ## Why
> One-file CLI support is not enough for a real media workflow.
> 
> ## Scope
> - queue model
> - worker mode
> - retry/resume
> - folder scan
> - watch mode
> - dashboard visibility
> 
> ## Acceptance criteria
> - multiple files can be queued and processed safely
> - failed jobs can be retried or resumed
> - folder scan can identify missing/weak subtitle cases
> - watch mode can enqueue new files
> - dashboard or status output exists for queued/failed/review states
> 
> ## Suggested child issues
> - Add processing queue and worker model
> - Add retry and resume support
> - Add recursive library scan
> - Add watch mode for new files
> - Add processing dashboard or status view
> 
> ## Dependencies
> - Add persistent state, artifact registry, and processing ledger
> - Build human review queue and local review UI</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
pr.author: Copilot
